script:
My sister had her first kid last year and gave me her old baby monitor “just in case I ever needed it.”
I didn’t have kids, but I figured it might be useful as a cheap indoor camera.
I set it up in my living room so I could check on my dog while I was away.
For months it worked perfectly.
Then one night, around 3 AM, I woke up to the monitor buzzing.
At first I thought my dog was barking or moving around.
But when I looked at the screen, my dog was asleep on the couch.
Still, I kept hearing faint noises.
A rhythmic tapping.
Then a soft whisper.
I couldn’t make out any words, so I turned the volume up.
The whisper became clearer.
A woman's voice saying, “Come here.”
I jumped up and ran to the living room.
No one was there.
My dog didn’t even wake up.
I unplugged the monitor and went back to bed shaken.
The next morning I checked its event log.
It showed a 19-second audio clip recorded at 3:06 AM.
When I played it back, the whisper was there again.
Only this time, at the end of the clip, the voice said,
“I know you can hear me now.”
I threw the monitor away that same day.
